explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **structured reporting of specific events involving authorities, institutions, or formal processes** (such as police incidents, legal proceedings, government statements, academic research findings, or celebrity news with official sources).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across multiple languages (English, Spanish, Indonesian, Finnish, Vietnamese, Portuguese) and domains including crime reports, political/legal controversies, scientific publications, entertainment news, and international incidents. It consistently activates on text that cites specific entities (e.g., RPD officers, the journal *Science*, the Supreme Court, Revolutionary Guards) while describing concrete actions or statements.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: High-activation texts contain detailed references to real-world events with named actors, institutions, or publications, often using formal or journalistic language. Low-activation examples are either generic (e.g., “The end of the year is a time to reflect”), fragmented, or lack specific institutional/authoritative framing (e.g., sports updates, error messages, routine announcements without cited sources).

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects linguistically structured descriptions of events anchored by institutional or authoritative context, focusing on semantic content that conveys factual reporting rather than general commentary or incomplete snippets. It appears to act as a “formal event reporter” filter across languages.

results.actual_text: Full profile →'"> The author is a Forbes contributor. The opinions expressed are those of the writer. China's stimulus program is prone
results.plain_text: Full profile →'"> The author is a Forbes contributor. The opinions expressed are those of the writer. China's stimulus program is prone to blow more bubbles in the economy next year. The government's latest infrastructure build-out may be a short-term boost for the stock market there, but it's perceived as a longer term negative considering the fact that Chinese provinces and the main government is going to be saddled with even more debt than it already has by building and investing in things it probably does not need.  "More wasteful investment means more destruction of capital and more bad debts," says Robin Parbrook, Schroders chief of Asian investment ex-Japan. Parbrook warned on Monday that China's "serial bubble-blowing" is a long-term problem.  The current government-driven infrastructure plan is your garden variety Chinese stimulus program of bridges to nowhere and high vacancy high rises. Most of the players are state-owned enterprises that get funded directly or indirectly by the government banks or by provincially-run public private partnerships. This could keep make the iShares FTSE China (FXI) exchange traded fund a loser in 2017 as it is laden with big state owned firms and banks. That ETF is actually up only 0.4% this year while the MSCI Emerging Markets is up by over 9%. The A-shares fund (ASHR) by Deutsche Bank has done even worse, down 14.7% year-to-date ending Dec. 19. That fund has more local focused companies in it, but they are also local-focused companies that many people in China haven't even heard of. Americans may be better off picking an active fund manager from an emerging market specialty shop, or pick a favorite China stock and roll with the punches.  More China volatility is expected next year. It is unclear yet what a Trump administration will do with China. Outside of Mexico, China is the country Trump is mostly willing to pick a fight with in 2017. Any significant trade disputes with the Chinese will be met with China lowering the value of its currency. Barclays Capital has been forecasting the renmimbi to end the year at 7 to the dollar, its weakest level in 8 years. It is currently 6.9544.  China has been relying on credit expansion to reach its economic growth forecasts of 6% for years now. These loans have had the effect of creating distortions and stresses in the banking system, contributing to bubbles in asset prices, not to mention oversupply in numerous sectors. Solar panels and automotive sector oversupply comes to mind.  This has also been a problem in real estate, still considered the preferred bank account of individuals in China.  Since the 2015 market crash following MSCI's rejection of including the A-shares in its emerging markets index, the Shanghai Composite has been an underperformer in the emerging market universe. China is growing, but its stock market is not. The Dow Jones Shanghai Index is off by 29% since May 1, 2015. FXI is down over 31%. ASHR is even worse, down 50.5% as that was the product most helped along by investors gambling on MSCI inclusion of Shanghai and Shenzhen listed stocks in its index. If MSCI included those shares, it would have created instant demand for those securities by index funds such as Deutsche Bank's China ETF. Locals that were burnt by investing in these securities on margin, thinking they were betting on a sure thing, have since opted out of the stock market.  But the bubble mentality created by cheap money is not dead yet in China. Bubble finance finds other ways to speculate and more recently this has been evident in property prices in the tier-1 cities like Shanghai, where price gains of 30-40% year-on-year have been reported. The Chinese authorities have taken steps to try and prevent this bubble from inflating further, and the latest data on property prices that has just been published shows that they have had some success. New home prices gained from the previous month in 55 of the 70 cities tracked by the government, compared with 62 in October. Local authorities have in recent months introduced restrictions on property purchases. They are now asking for bigger down payments for first-time home buyers.  At last Friday's economic planning conference in Beijing, the government highlighted in a statement that “houses should be built to be inhabited, not for investing.” The value of homes sold last month increased at their slowest pace so far this year.  "There are other challenges facing the Chinese authorities that might also contribute to a squeeze on liquidity and underpin higher borrowing costs," says Neil MacKinnon, an economist for VTB Capital in London. "This morning, the yuan one-month loan rate in Hong Kong climbed to an 11-month high and last week we saw a sharp sell-off in the Chinese bond market -- the biggest weekly loss since 2009 -- and the 10-year government bond yield moved up to 3.4%," he says, indicating that volatility is back in Chinese fixed income.  China’s corporate debt problem is widely reported. At some point, companies will require deleveraging, VTB's MacKinnon says.  Between 2009 to 2015, credit grew by 20% on average each year. The ratio of non-bank private credit-to-GDP rose from 150% to 200%. These non-bank credits come from China's famous "shadow banking" system, much of which is an instrument created by provincial governments to fund pet projects and other job creating endeavors and force investors into financial products that are lending to these dud companies.  According to the IMF, China's credit distortions are similar to those that led to painful deleveraging in Spain and Japan.  "We now expect that like Japan post-bubble, the Chinese government will use periodic fiscal stimulus packages to offset a sluggish and deflation-prone economy," says Parbrook. "This will lead to a rapid accumulation of government debt. The only difference being that the Chinese authorities may pretend the debt is held in the private sector, via the state owned enterprises and the local government investment vehicles," he says about the above-mentioned financial products that make up the bulk of China's shadow lending. "Large and accumulating bad debts in the banking system will end up on the government’s balance sheet. The lack of reform and serial bubble-blowing means long-term vulnerabilities are rising. This is among the biggest bubbles we will see in our investment careers," Parbrook warns.

predictions.text: "More wasteful investment means more destruction of capital and more bad debts," says Robin Parbrook, Schroders chief of Asian investment ex-Japan. Parbrook warned on Monday that China's "serial bubble-blowing" is a long-term problem.
predictions.reasoning: It directly quotes a specific financial institution representative issuing a warning about economic policy.

"We now expect that like Japan post-bubble, the Chinese government will use periodic fiscal stimulus packages to offset a sluggish and deflation-prone economy," says Parbrook.

results.actual_text: ifikasi Piala Dunia 2022, juga laga uji coba yang akan digelar mulai 25 Mei mendatang
results.plain_text: Gelandang timnas Indonesia berdarah Belanda, Marc Klok, harus pamit meninggalkan pemusatan latihan (TC) yang tengah dilangsungkan di Jakarta, sejak 1 Mei lalu. Klok memastikan hal ini pada Kamis (6/5) dinihari WIB.  Bukan tanpa alasan Klok harus pamit lebih dulu dari kamp latihan timnas Indonesia, dirinya memiliki urusan keluarga yang penting sehingga mau tak mau harus terbang ke Bali untuk urusan tersebut.  Ini merupakan kesempatan pertama Klok mengikuti TC timnas Indonesia, setelah dirinya resmi menjadi Warga Negara Indonesia (WNI). Gelandang milik Persija Jakarta ini sangat antusias, tapi tetap harus mengedepankan keluarga. "Pada hari kelima pemusatan latihan tim nasional Indonesia, saya terpaksa harus pergi lebih awal karena istri saya yang seorang diri di Bali akan melahirkan anak pertama kami dalam waktu dekat," tulis Klok, via Instagram pribadinya.  "Saya sangat senang mendapatkan kesempatan pertama untuk pemanggilan resmi timn nasional, dan ingin berterima kasih kepada Coach Shin Tae-yong dan tim yang telah memberikan saya kesempatan itu, dan mengizinkan saya untuk menemani istri untuk bersalin," sambung dia.    Klok hanya akan meninggalkan TC timnas Indonesia untuk sementara waktu, sampai anak pertamanya lahir dalam waktu dekat ini. Klok sendiri sangat menantikan momen ini, dan sudah mengumumkan anaknya berkelamin perempuan, beberapa waktu lalu.  Penampilan Klok selama turnamen pramusim Piala Menpora 2021 telah membawanya ke TC timnas Indonesia, sebagaimana Tae-yong mulai sadar akan kualitas Klok. Pada Piala Menpora, Klok membantu Persija menjuarai turnamen tersebut.  TC timnas Indonesia ini dilakukan demi menyambut lanjutan Kualifikasi Piala Dunia 2022, juga laga uji coba yang akan digelar mulai 25 Mei mendatang, di Uni Emirat Arab (UEA).  Tahun ini timnas Indonesia mencoba untuk kembali aktif, makanya mereka bakal melanjutkan TC Jakarta ini dengan TC luar negeri yang digelar di UEA. Pada TC luar negeri tersebut, timnas juga akan menjalani laga uji coba internasional.  Lawan yang akan dihadapi oleh Merah-Putih adalah Afganistan, serta Oman. Pertandingan akan digelar pada 25 dan 29 Mei. Lalu mereka akan memainkan lanjutan kualifikasi pada Juni, menghadapi Thailand, Vietnam, dan tuan rumah UEA, yang menjadi sisa lawan di Grup G.

"Saya sangat senang mendapatkan kesempatan pertama untuk pemanggilan resmi timn nasional, dan ingin berterima kasih kepada Coach Shin Tae-yong dan tim yang telah memberikan saya kesempatan itu, dan mengizinkan saya untuk menemani istri untuk bersalin," sambung dia.

results.actual_text: carry in the Bears' first two games behind quarterback Mitch Trubisky this season but has averaged 3.1 yards per carry in the five
results.plain_text: “Trust” was the buzzword David Montgomery used Thursday as he explained how he is trying to help the Chicago Bears running game climb out of a rut that has become more like a canyon over the last five games.“I think where I can really improve is just trusting it, just stop trying to do stuff on my own and just trusting that the guys in front of me and the play call is going to be there and where the path is going to be is going to hit,” Montgomery said. “I’ve got a lot of work to do. &quot;So that’s why I try to go back to the drawing board and try to fix what I can do as far as my running routes, my pass protection, my reads and my runs, just going back and reevaluating myself and making sure I clean up me first.”The second-year running back averaged 5 yards per carry in the Bears' first two games behind quarterback Mitch Trubisky this season but has averaged 3.1 yards per carry in the five games since Nick Foles took over midway through their Week 3 victory.Coach Matt Nagy said the explanation’ for why the Bears have failed to produce in the running game is “a little bit of everything.” That certainly includes the execution of Montgomery and the struggling line, the play calling, the changes to the offense with Foles at the helm and the tough run defenses of the Indianapolis Colts and Tampa Bay Buccaneers. But Nagy also noted that if they had the answer, they would be better.“It’s this close,” Montgomery said, holding up two fingers. “You’ve just got to keep trusting it, keep going to work every day and understanding that it’s going to pop, something’s going to happen. We’ve just got to keep fighting. That’s all you can do.”Rams defensive lineman Aaron Donald grabs Bears running back David Montgomery on a rush Monday in Inglewood, Calif. It should be noted that when offensive coordinator Bill Lazor was asked about Montgomery’s production this season, he led off with a story about one game in which Montgomery didn’t have great output on the ground yet still was awarded a game ball because of his effort and reliability in doing what he was asked to do.“In what I would consider the short time that I’ve been together with him, I feel like I found a guy that there isn’t anything you could ask him to do that he wouldn’t be ready to do,” Lazor said. “He doesn’t say much and he gets it done. I would say it’s unbelievable the amount of trust that we have in him.”It’s not likely to get any easier for the Bears this week as they face the New Orleans Saints, who have limited opponents to 89.7 rushing yards per game and 3.47 yards per carry, ranked fourth and third, respectivey, in the NFL.It would be understandable if Montgomery had blacked out last year’s game against the Saints from his mind. That’s when he had two carries for 6 yards in the Bears' infamous seven-carry game.“They’re a hell of a defense, guys that fly around,” Montgomery said. “I commend that defense for the way they play, the high motor in those guys. But last year things just didn’t go as planned for us, so this year we’ll have a better plan just going out playing hard and having fun.”It might help if the Bears found a second option behind Montgomery since Tarik Cohen tore the ACL in his right knee in Week 3. Cordarrelle Patterson has 25 carries for 71 yards (2.8 yards per rush) and had three carries for 1 yard Monday in the loss to the Los Angeles Rams.Veteran Lamar Miller has been on the practice squad for more than two weeks, and the Bears continue to monitor whether he’s ready to return to the game after missing all of last season with a torn ACL in his left knee.“Lamar is looking better and better each day, really each week,” Nagy said Wednesday. “It’s going to be a communication and trust process with Andre Tucker, our head trainer, just being able to see where he’s at with his leg. But I do think he’s getting close. He’s done everything really well as far as in meetings, in practice. Obviously, he’s a heck of a running back.”Montgomery said Miller also has helped him stay focused as the Bears try to get their running game on track.“He’s big on letting me know that you’ve just got to stick with it,” Montgomery said. “He’s being sure that I’m maintaining confidence in myself. He’s being sure that I’m having fun with the game just because he knows that it can all be gone. &quot;One play away, an injury away, anything can happen, so you want to just be sure that you take advantage of every opportunity and just remember to have fun with it because this is a game at the end of the day.”

predictions.text: “In what I would consider the short time that I’ve been together with him, I feel like I found a guy that there isn’t anything you could ask him to do that he wouldn’t be ready to do,” Lazor said. “He doesn’t say much and he gets it done. I would say it’s unbelievable the amount of trust that we have in him.”
predictions.reasoning: It quotes an offensive coordinator expressing trust in a player, reflecting an authoritative assessment within a sports institution.

results.actual_text: La jornada de este miércoles comenzó registrando sismos de med
results.plain_text: La jornada de este miércoles comenzó registrando sismos de mediana intensidad, en la zona norte del país. Aunque la mayoría de los movimientos resultan imperceptibles, estos ocurren debido a que Chile se encuentra ubicado en el límite de la placa tectónica de Nazca, donde impacta contra la Sudamericana. Por motivos de seguridad, resulta relevante mantenerse informado sobre los recientes sismos que se registran en el país, una labor a cargo del Centro Sismológico Nacional de la Universidad de Chile. Temblor hoy, miércoles 3 de enero en Chile Temblor hoy, miércoles 3 de enero en Chile Estos son los sismos reportados hoy por Sismología: 05:27 horas Temblor de magnitud 4.5, con epicentro a 69 km al oeste de Ollagüe y a unos 108 km de profundidad. 05:04 horas Informan de un sismo de magnitud 5.5, con epicentro a 131 km al este de Alto del Carmen y a una profundidad de 159 km. Senapred emitió un informe, indicando que el movimiento se percibió con las siguientes intensidades, en escala de Mercalli. Región de Atacama Copiapó: IV. Tierra Amarilla: IV. Alto del Carmen: III. Vallenar: III. Región de Coquimbo Andacollo: III. La Higuera: III. Río Hurtado: III. 04:04 horas Temblor de magnitud 3.5, ocurrido a 20 km al suroeste de Visviri y a una profundidad de 164 km. 00:14 horas Reportan un sismo de magnitud 3.9, localizado a 38 km al norte de Camiña y a 110 km de profundidad. ¿Qué hacer en caso de un sismo? ¿Qué hacer en caso de un sismo? El Servicio Nacional de Prevención y Respuesta ante Desastres entregó algunas recomendaciones sobre qué hacer cuando ocurre un movimiento sísmico. Estas son las sugerencias informadas por Senapred: Mantener la calma y buscar un lugar de protección sísmica. Protegerse debajo de un elemento firme. Si no es posible, mantenerse al lado del mismo objeto. Cortar la energía eléctrica y cerrar las llaves de paso de agua y gas. Si se está en la calle, alejarse de edificios, postes y cables eléctricos. En caso de estar en un evento masivo, la persona debe quedarse en el lugar y proteger la cabeza y cuello con los brazos. Además, también tendrá que seguir las indicaciones de los encargados de seguridad del recinto. Si se está manejando en un túnel, puente o autopista, se debe reducir la velocidad y detenerse tras salir del lugar.

Por motivos de seguridad, resulta relevante mantenerse informado sobre los recientes sismos que se registran en el país, una labor a cargo del Centro Sismológico Nacional de la Universidad de Chile.

results.actual_text: El 20 de marzo del año pasado, las Naciones Unidas patro
results.plain_text: El 20 de marzo del año pasado, las Naciones Unidas patrocinó un ránking con las naciones más felices del mundo en el marco del Día Internacional de la Felicidad. Para sorpresa de muchos, una ciudad peruana destacó en la lista: Huancayo. La capital de Junín, ubicada en el bello valle del Mantaro, fue fundada en 1572 y es una de las ciudades más altas del Perú a 3249 metros sobre el nivel del mar. La historia de Huancayo se remonta a muchos siglos atrás, pues fue el centro de la nación Wanka, que posteriormente fue territorio ocupado por los incas. En la ciudad podemos encontrar un gran legado cultural, lleno de vida, historia y celebraciones de distinto tipo. Visitar este territorio es una excelente oportunidad para apreciar sus paisajes y sitios arqueológicos, así como ser parte de sus tradiciones. Conoce cuatro de las más importantes: Carnavales En los meses de febrero y marzo se festejan los carnavales en Junín. Las diferentes provincias tienen sus propias celebraciones típicas como: el Ño Carnavalón en Huancayo, el carnaval jaujino, el carnaval tarmeño, el Huaylarsh carnaval, el carnaval marqueño, entre otros. En Huancayo el día central de los carnavales es el 7 de febrero e incluye una gran comparsa en la que participan instituciones públicas y privadas de toda la provincia. El Señor de Muruhuay Esta festividad se celebra en el centro poblado Muruhuay, en el distrito de Acobamba, perteneciente a la provincia de Tarma. En este lugar se encuentra pintado sobre una roca el Señor de Muruhuay. La tradición cuenta que en esta zona se encontró un manantial con aguas milagrosas que curaban enfermedades, y al remover las piedras se encontró -según relatos- una figura de Cristo crucificado en la que posteriormente se pintó al Cristo que conocemos. El día central de esta celebración es el 3 de mayo, aunque la fiesta suele realizarse todo el mes. Huaylarsh La danza típica de la región andina está relacionada con las épocas de siembre y cosecha. José María Arguedas define al Waylarsh como una danza “inmersa en el mito, fuerzas cósmicas y mágico religioso, sobre todo en el desarrollo y la vigencia de la cultura popular wanka”. Existe una versión del Waylarsh moderno que se centra en la celebración, la alegría y el cortejo. En el 2005, el Instituto Nacional de Cultura (Ministerio de Cultura) lo declaró como Patrimonio Cultural de la Nación. El Santiago Como cada año, la celebración en honor al apóstol Santiago se celebra el 24 de julio y culmina durante la primera semana de agosto. Huancayo es devota de esta figura religiosa, donde se le conoce como “taita Shanti”. En esta festividad se agradece la buena cosecha, buen clima, fecundidad de los animales y para alegrar al pueblo. Además, se construye un altar adornado con flores, ichu, mates de calabaza con harina de maíz, cintas multicolores y hojas de coca.

predictions.text: El 20 de marzo del año pasado, las Naciones Unidas patrocinó un ránking con las naciones más felices del mundo en el marco del Día Internacional de la Felicidad. Para sorpresa de muchos, una ciudad peruana destacó en la lista: Huancayo.
predictions.reasoning: It reports a UN-sponsored ranking event, directly involving an international institution and a specific outcome.
